Freya — scattering validated, scintillation unresolved (proof)

1 · Scattering fit is real

Data & model match in both bands; (data−model)/noise is white — CHIME χ²=1.34 std=1.16, DSA χ²=1.03 std=1.01; histograms on N(0,1). α=4.48, τ1GHz=0.115 ms. Caveat: CHIME lag-1=0.39 (mild residual temporal structure).

2 · Framework works — freya's null is physics, not a bug

Synthetic. Left: GP recovers injected Δνd along 1:1 (ratio 0.91). Middle: a RESOLVED scintle (12 MHz >> 2 MHz channel) gives a finite-width ACF. Right: an UNRESOLVED one (0.2 MHz < channel) collapses at lag 1 — exactly freya's signature.

3 · Scintillation is unresolved; coarse 5.5 MHz was contamination

[Superseded by panel 4: the host τ-screen IS detected via the m² ladder, and the 0.06 MHz here was a slope artifact.] ACF (col 2): gain ACF drops to noise within ~1 channel — no resolved scintle. m² vs 1/Δνchan (top-right): CHIME curves → multiple scales (broad spectral + sub-MHz), so coarse 'Δν_d=5.5 MHz' was the broadband part. Same-screen (bottom-right): predicted Δνd~10−⁴ MHz vs observed UL 0.3/0.06 MHz — ~1000× gap → host vs Galactic screens.

4 · Multi-screen test (up to 3 Lorentzians) — adversarially verified

Tested up to 3 superimposed Lorentzian scintle scales across a channelization ladder — DSA down to 0.74 MHz/ch at gain S/N=11 (there is no ‘2 MHz floor’). Four adversarial refutation agents stress-tested the result. (a) BIC prefers N=1: no 2nd/3rd scale (0/900 bootstrap refits prefer N=2; the ‘narrow’ component pins to the fit bound = a resolution artifact). (b) The single ~7–15 MHz width is NOT a common diffractive screen — it fails ν⁴ scaling by ~45× (DSA/CHIME=0.66 vs the required 30; implied α=−0.5, wrong sign), and ν⁴ from CHIME would demand a >340 MHz DSA scintle, wider than the band. (c) That width is a broadband ENVELOPE systematic, not a real scale: it collapses 15→3.9 MHz as the detrend degree rises and tracks the analysis span. (d) The host τ-screen IS detected indirectly — DSA m² rises 4.3× across the ladder (the unresolved signature); forward-modeling gives Δνd ~0.004–0.010 MHz, bracketing the τ-prediction 6.4×10−³ MHz (a resolved-7-MHz-only simulation gives a flat ladder — rejected). Verdict: freya has exactly ONE diffractive signature — an unresolved scintle consistent with the host screen — plus a non-scintillation spectral envelope; no second (Galactic) screen detected. This supersedes the ‘unresolved 0.06 MHz’, ‘broadband leakage’, and ‘upper limit, not a detection’ statements above. Caveats: the m² slope is robust (jackknife-stable) but the 5 points are nested re-binnings, so no clean σ is quoted; the unresolved Δνd is degenerate with modulation index (consistency with τ, not an independent measurement); the Galactic non-detection is at this sensitivity, not proof of absence.
